High-risk model family parser, artifact,.caveat: This is no-heavy source/static compatibility proof; live multi-turn output quality and speed rows remain separate.

Generation defaults, Native MTP, and VL.caveat: This proves no-heavy wiring, metadata ownership, and media/cache compatibility. Live MTP speed/equivalence and video/Omni quality remain separate live rows.

Qwen/JANG packaged MX matmul speed is re.caveat: Source/native-wheel speed and packaged-app speed are separate. A source pass does not clear a packaged compat-wheel PP review.

Qwen native MTP live decode speed and ou.caveat: This row covers decode speed/equivalence only. Prompt-processing throughput is tracked separately so MTP decode is not blamed for a broader Qwen/JANG PP floor failure.

Qwen 27B JANG_4M prompt-processing speed.caveat: The current clearance row is the post norm-format loader fix live default MTP/VLM artifact. Older native-MTP/VL prefill diagnostics remain in the details because they identify the prior slow/corrupt route; packaged-app parity is still covered by the packaged integrity gate and separate Qwen/JANG packaged speed row.

Ling/Bailing multilingual output quality.caveat: Current live artifacts show CJK leakage in Russian/non-CJK prompts or missing clearance evidence. Do not release-claim Ling/Bailing multilingual output quality yet.

Gemma4 26B CRACK Responses visible-conte.caveat: Current Gemma4 Responses thinking-budget artifact lacks visible assistant content or is missing. Do not treat HTTP 200, import success, or production-family PASS as Gemma4 visible quality clearance.

Gemma4 26B CRACK mixed-SWA app-engine sp.caveat: Current Gemma4 mixed-SWA bundled/app-engine artifacts do not all meet the 80 tok/s decode floor. Do not claim Gemma4 heterogeneous SWA speed clearance from one cold sample.

Cross-family live multi-turn smoke matri.caveat: Current live all-local smoke coverage is incomplete. Do not claim broad family support until DSV4, Gemma4, Hy3, Ling/Bailing, MiniMax, MiMo-V2, Nemotron, Qwen3.6, ZAYA text, and ZAYA-VL live rows pass cache, recall, visible-output, and gibberish checks.

MiMo V2.5 JANG_2L runtime/tool/long-prom.caveat: MiMo V2.5 JANG_2L has current structural, narrow text/cache, selected-expert parity, exact CB cache/L2 proof, native thinking-off prompt proof, and the current JANGTQ2 packaged decode-speed floor when the audit reports it. Remaining open items: corrected MiMo artifact/upload or runtime decode proof, tool/JSON literal exactness, decode speed, VL/audio/video wiring. Do not release-clear MiMo from short smokes or speed-only proof.

model_upload_action_reasons: JANGTQ_2 current artifact fails plain, structured JSON, and parsed tool-argument literal exactness before parser or JSON repair; rebuild/reupload with a less lossy literal-safe profile unless source-vs-quant proves a runtime decode bug

N2 Pro 397B JANG1L/JANGTQ runtime/cache/.caveat: N2 Pro 397B JANG1L/JANGTQ is now tracked as an explicit release blocker. The current no-heavy parser/cache/family policy contracts are present, the local JANG_1L model/index preflight is registered, and current-source plus real Electron dev-app probes have loaded JANG_1L on the 128 GB host. Treat JANG_1L as careful-RAM live-proof work, not permanent model infeasibility; do not sign, notarize, tag, or publish a release claiming N2 support until both quant profiles pass the same live runtime/cache/API/UI gates as the other release-critical families.

local_artifact_probe.boundary: The local N2 JANG_1L artifact/index is registered, but the current memory preflight is only a launch-safety warning. JANG_1L should be treated as careful-RAM live-proof work, not permanent infeasibility; this is not live runtime proof.

harness_contract.prompt: Reply with exactly N2_JANG1L_VISIBLE and no other text.

assertion_failures: final visible assistant content is empty
UI turn ended with empty visible assistant content

jang1l_real_ui_one_turn.release_boundary: This one-turn proof avoids the prior second-turn working-set guard but still fails visible output: N2 JANG_1L streamed whitespace only. It proves live load/cache/L2/autodetect surfaces, not release support, multi-turn cache reuse, tools, Responses, or visible quality.

red.second_turn_error: Metal GPU working set too full (102% of 107.5GB cap)

boundary: This proves N2 JANG_1L can load and execute one bounded real Electron dev-app Chat request.
It does not prove usable visible output quality: the first assistant content was whitespace/empty.
It does not prove multi-turn cache reuse: the second turn was rejected by the Metal working-set guard at 102% of the 107.5GB cap.
It does not clear tools, Responses, Responses stream, L2 restart, media, installed-app parity, public tunnel parity, package/sign/notarize/tag/upload/download, or release support.

jangtq2_live_proof.boundary: Current-source N2 JANGTQ2 chat/cache/Responses proof only. This does not clear N2 JANG_1L, installed-app/UI parity, media rows, or full release support.

jangtq2_l2_restart_proof.boundary: Current-source N2 JANGTQ2 fresh-process L2 restart proof only. This does not clear N2 JANG_1L, installed-app/UI parity, media rows, same-model tunnel parity, or full release support.

assistant_messages: Done — this is the second UI turn. REAL_UI_LIVE_TOOL_TWO

red_followup.boundary: A stricter custom prompt still failed: the second turn did not create real_ui_tool_probe_2.txt and visible output degenerated into repeated exclamation marks after a tool_choice-required error. Treat this as a stricter prompt/model quality red row, not as the default checkpoint proof.

jangtq2_real_ui_prevresp_proof.boundary: Current Electron dev-app N2 JANGTQ2 default Responses tool/cache proof with previous_response_id continuation. This supports the checkpoint candidate profile but does not clear stricter long-delta prompts, audio, public tunnel parity, N2 JANG_1L, or full release support.

checks.release_boundary: This proof captures direct/gateway raw SSE for N2 JANGTQ2 Responses tool + tool-result continuation. It does not prove installed app, public tunnel, media, or release readiness.

jangtq2_responses_stream_boundary.boundary: Direct server and panel gateway raw SSE proof for N2 JANGTQ2 Responses required tool plus tool-result continuation. This does not clear public tunnel parity, audio, N2 JANG_1L, or full release support.

release_blocker.next_proof: Obtain reporter installed app bundle hash provenance matching a public/local vMLX server.py route proof, or refresh reporter parity metadata against a known public DMG; do not rerun broad cache/model probes until this provenance gap changes.

Do not claim the real UI cross-family matrix is release-cleared until each target family is exercised through the current dev app UI with a real loaded model, real chat settings, tool/reasoning display, cache reuse, and no parser leakage.

direct_off_exactness_boundary.root_boundary: Direct/off DSV4 exact-code generation fails under thinking_closed while requested-thinking exactness passes under thinking_open; hidden force-on does not clear explicit direct/off quality.

exact_code_root_boundary.root_boundary: Current DSV4 exact-code evidence points at explicit direct/off rail exact-code generation, not template mismatch, hidden reasoning leakage, prefix cache, forced sampling controls, or bundle defaults. Requested-thinking success is diagnostic only; the current logit boundary is at THREE. / identifier continuation, and live prompt-boundary bisection is same-rail wording-sensitive but its passing rows are not direct/off clearance when they run on the effective thinking-open rail.
